subject: [PATCH] tracing: Fix context switch counter truncation

reviews.author: Masami Hiramatsu (Google)
reviews.summary: Identified the patch as correct and provided an Acked-by tag, indicating that it can be applied to the kernel.

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the tracing system where context switch counter truncation occurs due to an unsigned int being assigned to a u64 variable, causing lost bits and incorrect reporting of changes.

reviews.author: Masami Hiramatsu (Google)
reviews.summary: Identified the patch as a fix for the context switch counter truncation issue and provided an ACK with Acked-by.

2026-07-18.patch_summary: The patch fixes a context switch counter truncation issue in the tracing subsystem, where the sampled count is stored as an unsigned long long to preserve its full value.

reviews.author: Masami Hiramatsu (Google)
reviews.summary: Identified the patch as a correct fix for the context switch counter truncation issue and acknowledged it with an Acked-by tag.

reviews.author: Breno Leitao
reviews.summary: Confirmed that the unsigned int cnt truncation is real and reviewed the patch with a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
On Fri, Jul 17, 2026 at 10:32:52AM -0700, Usama Arif wrote:
> This affects long-running systems and workloads with high context-switch
> rates. A CPU switching 1,000 times per second takes about 50 days.

nr_context_switches_cpu() returns "unsigned long long"
and rq->nr_switches is u64, so the unsigned int cnt truncation is real

> Fixes: 64cf7d058a00 ("tracing: Have trace_marker use per-cpu data to read user space")
> Cc: stable@vger.kernel.org
> Reported-by: Breno Leitao <leitao@debian.org>
> Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>

Reviewed-by: Breno Leitao <leitao@debian.org>

Thanks for the investigation and fix,
--breno

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch fixes a context switch counter truncation issue in the Linux kernel's tracing subsystem. The problem occurs when a CPU performs more than 2^32 context switches, causing the counter to lose its upper bits and report incorrect changes. To fix this, the sampled count is stored as an unsigned long long instead of an unsigned int, preserving the full value.
